summary: Round-2 regression is closed. The stale-closure hazard is eliminated by removing the redundant guard in ChatScreen.ReadyContent.callbacks.onSend — nothing remains inside the remembered lambda that could capture a stale value. `sendRef` is a `rememberUpdatedState(onSend)` (live State), and `text` is read through the `by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf("") }` property delegate, which dereferences the MutableState on every invocation — so both reads refresh on each tap. The text-clear after an accidental early-return is benign: `MultimodalInputBar.canSend = (state.text.isNotBlank() || state.hasAttachments) && !state.isGenerating` consumes the fresh `inputState` and gates IconButton.enabled, so the onSend lambda cannot fire when both text and attachments are empty. Even if it somehow did, `ChatViewModel.send()` short-circuits at `if (normalized.isEmpty() && pending.isEmpty()) return` (ChatViewModel.kt:107), and clearing empty text to empty is a no-op. All round-1 blockers remain closed: attachments are snapshotted and split by type in send() before being cleared, runInference receives List<Bitmap>/List<ByteArray> matching the helper signature, ThumbnailStrip keys by `attachment.id`, MultimodalInputBar is `(state, callbacks, modifier)` with @Immutable/@Stable holders, `rememberSaveable` preserves the draft text, Attachment.id via AtomicLong, trim centralised in VM, FQNs imported, dead symbols removed. Deferred items (onCleared scope, MessageList double-effect, Failed Scaffold) correctly not re-raised per round-3 scope.
